title: Error executing workflow due to undefined properties
symptom: When attempting to execute a workflow, an error occurs stating 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'execute')'. This prevents any workflows from running successfully.
not_this_if: Error occurs in a different node or environment
root_cause: This error typically arises when there is a mismatch in the configuration between the primary n8n instance and the worker node. Specifically, if the worker node is not set up with the same Docker image/version or execution mode, it can lead to undefined properties during workflow execution.
fix_steps: Ensure that both the primary and worker nodes are using the same Docker image/version.
Set the environment variable EXECUTIONS_MODE=queue on both the primary and worker nodes.
Verify that both nodes share the same N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY, database connection, and Redis connection.
Set N8N_REINSTALL_MISSING_PACKAGES=true on both primary and worker.
Update to the latest version of n8n.
agent_instruction: Check and synchronize the configuration between the primary and worker nodes.
